chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ جَاوَزَ مِيقَاتَ أَرْضِهِ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ أَوْ دَخَلَ مَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 76 – The one who exceeds the land of a juncture without Ihraam, or enters Makkah without Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/244)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/244)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ مَرَّ عَلَى الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي يُحْرِمُ النَّاسُ مِنْهُ فَنَسِيَ أَوْ جَهِلَ فَلَمْ يُحْرِمْ حَتَّى أَتَى مَكَّةَ فَخَافَ إِنْ رَجَعَ إِلَى الْوَقْتِ أَنْ يَفُوتَهُ الْحَجُّ فَقَالَ يَخْرُجُ مِنَ الْحَرَمِ وَ يُحْرِمُ وَ يُجْزِئُهُ ذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about a man who passed by the juncture which the people were wearing Ihraam from, but he forgot, or did not know. So he did not wear Ihraam until he came to Makkah. Then, he feared that if he were to return to the juncture, he would miss the Hajj. So heasws said: ‘He should go out from the Harram, and he should wear Ihraam, and that would suffice him’.
en.sarwar: 6. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who passed through Miqat (place for assuming the state of Ihram). He forgot or was ignorant; so he did not assume the state of Ihram until he entered Makkah, then he was afraid of not being able to come back in time for al-Hajj . He (the Imam) said, ‘He must go outside al-Haram (the sacred area) to assume the state of Ihram and it is sufficient.’”
